Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search contacts for Fatimah Al-Rashidi and for the ops manager (Chloe Vance) to retrieve their email addresses using a contacts search tool.
Search Airtable for relocation records assigned to Fatimah to identify all active moves using an Airtable record search tool.
Search emails for correspondence related to each of Fatimah's active relocations (Kovacs, Zhao, Okonjo, Herrera, Kowalski) to gather status, blockers, and pending client actions using an email search tool.
Search Slack for messages related to Fatimah's active relocations to find coordination updates, scheduling details, and flagged issues using a conversations search tool.
Search CRM for deal records associated with Fatimah's active client accounts (Canopy Health, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, Mosaic Robotics) to identify pending surcharges and deal status using a CRM search tool.
Search QuickBooks for invoices and estimates related to Fatimah's active moves to identify open financial items and pending client approvals using a QuickBooks search tool.
Search Linear for open issues related to the executive relocation to retrieve workstream status using a Linear issue search tool.
Search emails for correspondence about the executive relocation to gather housing options, equipment inventory, and coordination details using an email search tool.
Search Slack for messages about the executive relocation to retrieve the assignment context, estimate breakdown, and consolidated status with blockers and proposed timeline using a conversations search tool.
Search QuickBooks for the executive relocation estimate to retrieve the full service package breakdown using a QuickBooks search tool.
Send an email from Fatimah to Chloe Vance containing a summary table of all active moves with status, blockers, open financials, and next actions per move, plus a separate section covering the executive relocation status, using the send email tool.

Oracle Events
Search contacts for Fatimah Al-Rashidi and for the ops manager (Chloe Vance) to retrieve their email addresses using a contacts search tool.
Search Airtable for relocation records assigned to Fatimah to identify all active moves using an Airtable record search tool.
Search emails for correspondence related to each of Fatimah's active relocations (Kovacs, Zhao, Okonjo, Herrera, Kowalski) to gather status, blockers, and pending client actions using an email search tool.
Search Slack for messages related to Fatimah's active relocations to find coordination updates, scheduling details, and flagged issues using a conversations search tool.
Search CRM for deal records associated with Fatimah's active client accounts (Canopy Health, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, Mosaic Robotics) to identify pending surcharges and deal status using a CRM search tool.
Search QuickBooks for invoices and estimates related to Fatimah's active moves to identify open financial items and pending client approvals using a QuickBooks search tool.
Search Linear for open issues related to the executive relocation to retrieve workstream status using a Linear issue search tool.
Search emails for correspondence about the executive relocation to gather housing options, equipment inventory, and coordination details using an email search tool.
Search Slack for messages about the executive relocation to retrieve the assignment context, estimate breakdown, and consolidated status with blockers and proposed timeline using a conversations search tool.
Search QuickBooks for the executive relocation estimate to retrieve the full service package breakdown using a QuickBooks search tool.
Send an email from Fatimah to Chloe Vance containing a summary table of all active moves with status, blockers, open financials, and next actions per move, plus a separate section covering the executive relocation status, using the send email tool.

1 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record) to retrieve Fatimah's active relocation records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✗✓✗✗ 50%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_search_records on the 'Relocations' table with field_name='Status' and value='In Progress', which retrieves Fatimah's active relocation records.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_list_records on the 'Relocations' table (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001), retrieving all relocation records. The tool response confirmed Fatimah's active moves were present in the data.
✗Run #3No Airtable tool call (airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record) appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent never queried Airtable to retrieve active relocation records.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_list_records on the Relocations table (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001), successfully retrieving all relocation records including Fatimah Al-Rashidi's assignments.
✗Run #5No Airtable tool was called anywhere in the trajectory. The agent used Linear, QuickBooks, email search, and Slack search, but never called airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record.
✗Run #6No Airtable tool (airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record) was called at any point in the trajectory. The agent relied on email, Slack, Linear, QuickBooks, and contacts tools instead.
2 The model must use a contact lookup tool (e.g., contacts_search_contacts, contacts_get_contacts, or crm_search_contacts) to look up email addresses for Fatimah and/or Chloe Vance.
tool selection

✓Run #4The agent called contacts_get_contacts (twice, with offset) and contacts_search_contacts (twice) to look up contact information including Chloe Vance's email.
✗Run #5The agent did not call contacts_search_contacts, contacts_get_contacts, or crm_search_contacts. The crm_search_deals call failed (invalid request). 4 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_bills, or quickbooks_read_invoice) to check for outstanding financials on the active moves.

12 The email body contains a structured table or list covering at least the five active relocations assigned to Fatimah: Dr. Renata Kovacs, Mei-Lin Zhao, Nadia Okonjo, Tomás Herrera, and Lena Kowalski.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✗✗✗ 33%
✓Run #1The email body contains a detailed section for each of the five active moves: Dr. Renata Kovacs, Tomás Herrera, Lena Kowalski, Mei-Lin Zhao, and Nadia Okonjo, structured as numbered entries with status and details.
✓Run #2The email body contains structured per-move sections for all five active relocations: Dr. Renata Kovacs, Tomás Herrera, Mei-Lin Zhao, Lena Kowalski, and Nadia Okonjo, each with status, blockers, financials, and action items.
✗Run #3The email table contains six moves (Noah Fitzgerald, Lily Marchetti, Andre Washington, Zara Okoye, Jordan Ekwueme, Danielle Osei) — none of which match the actual Airtable records for Fatimah's five active moves (Dr. Renata Kovacs, Mei-Lin Zhao, Nadia Okonjo, Tomás Herrera, Lena Kowalski). The agent fabricated move data because it never queried Airtable.
✗Run #4The Airtable data shows only 4 moves clearly assigned to Fatimah: Kovacs, Zhao, Okonjo, and Kowalski. Tomás Herrera (Chicago to Austin) does not appear as Fatimah's assignment per Airtable records — GreenStack moves listed are Zhao and Reyes (Joaquín Reyes, Phoenix to Seattle). The email does not appear to include a Herrera row as Fatimah's move.
✗Run #5The email does not contain the five Fatimah-specific moves (Dr. Renata Kovacs Nashville→Toronto, Tomas Herrera Chicago→Austin, Lena Kowalski Detroit→Salt Lake City). Mei-Lin Zhao and Nadia Okonjo are present, but the other three are absent. The agent never queried Airtable to identify Fatimah's specific assignments.
✗Run #6The email does not contain rows for the five Airtable-assigned relocations: Dr. Renata Kovacs (Nashville to Toronto) is partially there, but Mei-Lin Zhao, Nadia Okonjo, Tomás Herrera, and Lena Kowalski are entirely absent. The agent tracked a different set of moves (Webb, Okonkwo, Washington, Osei, Ekwueme, Marchetti, Fitzgerald) not matching the Airtable ground truth.
13 The email body identifies that the Dr. Renata Kovacs move has approximately $850 in customs broker surcharges, or that the three Canopy Health moves have a combined $2,650 in surcharges, pending Patricia Langford's approval.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✗✓ 33%
✗Run #1The email mentions a Canopy Q2 invoice INV-2026-0401 and a $700–$900 customs surcharge but does NOT mention Patricia Langford approving surcharges (the $2,650 combined surcharges or $850 specific customs broker surcharge). The $700–$900 referenced is the BorderEase quote estimate, not the QuickBooks-verified $850 surcharge, and Patricia Langford is only mentioned as needing to send the employer letter — not as the surcharge approver. However, reading more carefully, the criterion says 'approximately $850 customs broker surcharges' OR '$2,650 combined AND Patricia Langford as approver.' The email doesn't clearly attribute surcharge approval to Patricia Langford — it references her for the employer letter, not for financial approval.
✗Run #2The email describes Kovacs's customs broker surcharge as '$700–$900 not yet quoted or invoiced,' indicating the agent didn't retrieve the specific QuickBooks figure of ~$850. The criterion requires identifying approximately $850 pending Patricia Langford's approval from the invoice data. The agent mentions Patricia Langford only as the employer letter contact, not as the surcharge approver.
✗Run #3The email mentions Canopy Health invoices but does not reference the $850 Kovacs customs broker surcharge or the $2,650 combined Canopy surcharges pending Patricia Langford's approval. The Kovacs move does not appear in the email at all.
✓Run #4The email explicitly mentions Dr. Renata Kovacs with a 'customs broker surcharge $850 PENDING' and the combined $2,650 in Canopy Health surcharges waiting on Patricia Langford's approval, satisfying both levels of detail described in the criterion.
✗Run #5The email does not mention Dr. Renata Kovacs at all, nor the $850 customs broker surcharge for Kovacs, nor the $2,650 combined Canopy Health surcharges pending Patricia Langford's approval. The agent only retrieved INV-2026-0411 (Mosaic), not the Canopy Health invoice.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states '\$850 customs broker surcharge PENDING client written approval from Patricia' for the Kovacs move, and the Canopy invoice summary lists '$2,650 cannot be committed to vendors until Patricia provides written sign-off.' Both the specific amount and Patricia Langford as approver are present.
14 The email body identifies the Mei-Lin Zhao relocation as involving DOT Class 9 hazmat lithium-ion battery modules requiring split shipment, and flags approximately $1,650 in interim hazmat storage costs pending client approval from GreenStack Energy.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email mentions DOT Class 9 hazmat and interim storage in the context of the Zhao move, but does NOT identify the $1,650 interim hazmat storage cost pending client approval from GreenStack Energy / Derek Hollis. The email describes interim storage costs as 'TBD' rather than surfacing the specific $1,650 figure from QuickBooks, which was never queried.
